summary: A symmetrical rectangular courtyard structure with a central elongated building capped by a stepped wooden/gable-like roof and four tall stone towers at the corners. White low walls/blocks outline the perimeter inside a larger dirt area. The central building has layered horizontal banding of darker and lighter blocks, staggered stair-like wooden roof sections, and small glass windows. Towers are tall dark stone columns topped with layered flat platforms and sea-lantern-like lights.

shape.overall_form: central elongated hall inside a rectangular walled courtyard with four corner towers
shape.symmetry: axisymmetric along the long centerline (bilateral symmetry)

materials.name: brick
materials.role: darker horizontal banding on central building (possible andesite/stonebrick variant)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: roof/platform edging on towers and central trim

materials.name: slab_wood
materials.role: wooden platform layers on tower tops

elements: central elongated hall with stepped wooden roof
four corner towers with layered flat platforms and light block on top
low white perimeter blocks forming inner boundary
small square windows in towers and central building
rebuild_hints: Place a rectangular courtyard roughly 21x41 blocks with a one-block-high white block border inset from the outer dirt edge.

Build four identical stonebrick towers near each corner inside the white border; towers ~12-14 blocks tall with small windows spaced vertically and a three-layer flat platform roof (stone slab outer edge, wood slab inner) topped by a light block.

Construct the central building along the courtyard long axis: two-story banded walls (alternate dark stone band and light white band), a central entrance projection, and a stepped wooden gable roof made from stairs to create the curved stepped profile.
Use glass panes for the horizontal window strips on the central building and single-pane/blocks for tower windows. Add slab stone edging at roof eaves and white vertical posts as accents.
uncertainties: Exact block types for darker banding on central walls: appears as stonebrick or andesite/brick; identified as 'brick' with moderate uncertainty.
Precise internal floor count and internal layout are not visible; floors_estimate set to 2 based on exterior banding and window rows.
Exact dimensions are approximate from image perspective; width/depth/height are rounded to nearby integers.
